title: FISCAL SYSTEM v. Pahlavi Period
units: narrative with cited statistical figures (tax shares of revenue/GNP, budget composition)
license_terms: Encyclopaedia Iranica -- academic reference, cite author/article per their citation guidance
notes: Article by Massoud Karshenas (Vol. IX, Fasc. 6, pp. 651-654; published Dec 15, 1999; last updated Oct 12, 2012). Covers the modern Persian/Iranian fiscal system's development across three periods: 1921-41 (Millspaugh mission tax rationalization, 1931 trade monopoly law, indirect-tax-dominated revenue, ~70% of revenue from indirect taxation, oil only 10-16% of revenue); 1941-54 (WWII disruption, first Seven-Year Plan financed partly by a World Bank loan that was withheld during the oil nationalization dispute); 1954-79 (oil revenue share rising from 15-16% to >75% of government revenue by 1975-77, low direct-tax effort, rising military share of expenditure from 28% to 48% of budget 1963-77, and Farhad Mehran's 1971 incidence analysis showing net-negative distributional impact of tax/expenditure policy despite oil-funded largesse). Complements the economy-ix-pahlavi-period article (also cites Karshenas's 1990 book extensively). Retrieved via browser tool (Cloudflare bot-challenge blocked plain curl/WebFetch, same pattern as the other two Iranica articles) and get_page_text extraction, saved as clean plain text.
